stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the continued relevance of large language models (LLMs) from Meta.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformer, llama, and AI/ML communities.

stories.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to seal 2FA code from users
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability in Copilot allowed attackers to intercept or seal Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) codes.
The exploit highlights systemic failures in the current security practices surrounding Large Language Models (LLMs).
The incident serves as a major warning about the security risks inherent in rapidly deploying AI tools.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers and deepseek_v3 architectures.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML components like transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests tracking trending or benchmarked large language models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance gains.
This update specifically targets and optimizes performance on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: The Microsoft Surface Laptop 8 and Surface Pro 12 now come with Snapdragon X2 chips
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: Microsoft is updating its Surface Laptop and Surface Pro lines with the new Snapdragon X2 chips.
This transition follows previous models that used the Snapdragon X1, continuing Microsoft's Windows on Arm strategy.
The hardware refresh supports and advances Microsoft's Copilot Plus PC initiative.

stories.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
stories.author: Michelle Chan and Tim Bradshaw, Financial Times
bullet_points: Nvidia is planning a substantial debt issuance, aiming to raise over $25 billion.
The bond deal is significant as it is the first major debt offering by Nvidia since 2021.
The move tests investor appetite for continued high exposure to the rapidly expanding AI sector.

stories.title: Fox’s $22B Roku acquisition aims to expand its reach into smart TVs, advertising
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Fox is planning a major acquisition of Roku valued at $22 billion.
The acquisition aims to significantly expand Fox's presence in the smart TV and streaming market.
Fox intends to gain control over Roku's streaming hardware, operating system, and FAST services.

stories.title: FusionRS: A Large-Scale RGB-Infrared Remote Sensing Dataset for Dual-Modal Vision-Language Foundation Models
stories.author: Jiujiang Guo
bullet_points: Introduces FusionRS, a new large-scale dataset combining RGB and Infrared remote sensing imagery.
Addresses a gap in current remote sensing AI by integrating infrared data, which offers unique thermal and illumination-invariant features.
Aims to enhance Vision-Language Foundation Models for Earth observation by leveraging dual-modal (RGB-IR) input.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the high inference costs associated with context accumulation in long-horizon LLM agent sessions.
It tackles the issue where traditional context management methods (like pruning/eviction) cause cache invalidation due to sequence mutations.
The framework introduces a dual-granularity context management approach to balance text sparsity with prompt cache continuity.

stories.title: ActiveSAM: Image-Conditional Class Pruning for Fast and Accurate Open-Vocabulary Segmentation
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: ActiveSAM addresses the inefficiency of using large models like SAM 3 for Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation (OVSS).
It introduces a training-free, zero-shot inference framework to convert SAM 3 into an active-vocabulary segmenter.
The core innovation involves pruning the decoding process to only focus on the active subset of classes present in each image.

stories.title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
stories.author: Wenhai Sun
bullet_points: Challenges the assumption that Differential Privacy (DP) inherently protects Federated Learning (FL) against backdoor attacks.
Demonstrates a fundamental tension: DP compliance can mask the statistical signatures needed to detect malicious updates.
Suggests that while bypassing DP allows for current defenses, adhering to DP inadvertently weakens security against specific attacks.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the high inference costs associated with context accumulation in long-horizon LLM agent sessions.
It tackles the problem where traditional context management methods (like pruning/eviction) cause cache invalidation due to sequence mutations.
The framework introduces a dual-granularity approach to balance text sparsity with prompt cache continuity.

stories.title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
stories.author: Jack Lindsey
bullet_points: Researchers investigated if language models internally track the 'value' of their current strategy (likelihood of achieving goals).
A 'value' axis was constructed for Qwen3-8B using synthetic in-context reinforcement learning data.
Activations along this axis correlate with verbalized confidence, backtracking behavior, and code correctness.

stories.title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
stories.author: Xingyu Fu
bullet_points: Introduces ContextRL, a novel context-aware Reinforcement Learning (RL) method for LLMs.
Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: difficulty in identifying small, decisive evidence within long or complex contexts (e.g., tool traces, images).
Improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance by using an indirect auxiliary objective during training.

stories.title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
stories.author: Qingyao Ai
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaSyn,' a new dataset of 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio journals.
The dataset is designed to benchmark LLM agents on complex, multi-stage scientific reasoning tasks (retrieval, selection, synthesis).
Addresses a gap in existing benchmarks by providing ground truth across the entire systematic review pipeline.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the high inference costs associated with context accumulation in long-horizon LLM agent sessions.
It tackles the problem where existing context management methods (pruning/eviction) cause cache invalidation due to sequence mutations.
The framework introduces a dual-granularity approach to balance text sparsity with prompt cache continuity.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: lwhsiao
bullet_points: A security vulnerability (backdoor) was discovered in LinkedIn's job offer system.
This represents a significant security risk within a major professional networking platform.
The story highlights the ongoing threat of sophisticated attacks targeting corporate/professional infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model responses.
The updated engine also offers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, integrating with popular local LLM runners.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms for this functionality is planned for the near future.

stories.title: Mobileye is entering the US robotaxi market with standalone service
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Mobileye is planning to enter the US robotaxi market.
The service will utilize its existing Moovit platform.
The planned launch date for the service in a US city is 2027.

stories.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The UK is considering legislation to ban social media access for children under the age of 16.
The proposed measures may include the implementation of overnight curfews for minors.
Critics argue that such bans could drive children toward riskier online alternatives or circumvent them using VPNs.

stories.title: A Chinese rocket breaks apart dangerously close to the Starlink constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: A Chinese rocket experienced a dangerous breakup near the Starlink constellation.
The incident is estimated to have created between 100 and 150 new pieces of space debris.
The event highlights ongoing risks associated with space debris and satellite constellations.

stories.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The research investigates whether modern neural networks (PRISM2D, GFNet, ViT-B/16) exhibit the phase-sensitivity asymmetry observed in natural images.
It tests the hypothesis, based on Oppenheim and Lim (1981), that image identity is more encoded in the Fourier phase than the magnitude.
The study uses a causal intervention method—transplanting phase between images—to determine which input the classifier's prediction follows.

stories.title: TuneJury: An Open Metric for Improving Music Generation Preference Alignment
stories.author: Chris Donahue
bullet_points: Introduces TuneJury, an open, instance-level pairwise reward model specifically for text-to-music generation.
The model predicts a music preference score based on a text prompt and an audio clip, aiding in preference alignment.
It is trained on diverse public human-preference labels, including arena-style votes and expert aesthetic ratings.

stories.title: Bayesian Inference and Decision Audits for Public Archives of Frontier AI Evaluations
stories.author: Yanan Long
bullet_points: The paper critiques the common practice of viewing public AI evaluations (like leaderboards) as definitive, static measures.
It proposes framing the collection of AI evaluation data as a Bayesian inference problem to account for biases from reporting rules, benchmark changes, and missing data.
It analyzes longitudinal records from various benchmarks (LiveBench, Open LLM Leaderboard v2, LMArena, GAIA, tau-bench) to provide a more nuanced view of AI progress.

stories.title: When in Doubt, Plan It Out: Committed Small Language Model Deliberation for Reactive Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Odinaldo Rodrigues
bullet_points: Introduces PACT, a hybrid architecture designed to improve Reinforcement Learning (RL) robustness in unfamiliar environments.
Combines a fast, reactive RL policy with a slow, deliberative Small Language Model (SLM) planner for decision-making.
The SLM asynchronously generates and validates action plans, which are executed directly if deemed safe and feasible.

stories.title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
stories.author: Sunghwan Hong
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) by focusing on 3D geometry for robot policy learning.
Proposes a 'Geometric Action Model' to handle complex, contact-rich manipulation in the physical world.
Aims to bridge the gap between high-level semantic understanding (from foundation models) and low-level 3D physical reasoning.

stories.title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes
stories.author: Hongsheng Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in online Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of Vision-Language Agents (VLAs).
Proposes 'Hierarchical Advantage Weighting' to provide richer, per-transition supervision instead of relying on a single binary outcome.
Aims to overcome the limitation where conflating sparse outcomes into a single scalar signal limits learning guidance after basic task success is achieved.

stories.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The research investigates whether modern neural network classifiers (PRISM2D, GFNet, ViT-B/16) exhibit the phase-sensitivity asymmetry observed in natural images.
It tests the hypothesis, based on Oppenheim-Lim (1981), that image identity is encoded more strongly in the phase of the Fourier transform than in its magnitude.
The study uses a causal intervention method—transplanting phase between two images—to determine which original image the classifier's prediction follows.

stories.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of post-hoc context erasing in LLMs due to the global propagation of cached states (KV cache).
Highlights the practical problem where local edits (like removing stale facts or injections) require expensive recomputation of all subsequent tokens.
Introduces 'KVEraser' as a method to efficiently steer the KV cache for localized context removal.

stories.title: ExpRL: Exploratory RL for LLM Mid-Training
stories.author: Aviral Kumar
bullet_points: Introduces ExpRL, a new approach using exploratory Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM improvement during mid-training.
Addresses a limitation of current RL methods, which rely heavily on manually curated reasoning traces to teach primitive skills.
Aims to improve LLM reasoning by making the learning process less dependent on pre-specifying exact skills.

stories.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of post-hoc context erasing in LLMs due to the global propagation of cached state influence.
Proposes 'KVEraser' to learn how to steer the KV cache for efficient, localized context removal.
Relevant for long-context LLM applications where stale facts or harmful injections require mid-stream correction.

stories.title: DEEPRUBRIC: Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision for Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Jiyan He
bullet_points: Introduces DEEPRUBRIC, a method using Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision to enhance Deep Research Agents.
The research addresses the inefficiency of current RL approaches where rubric quality depends on accurate task scope inference by the LLM.
It aims to improve the efficiency and reliability of RL-based agents that synthesize long-form reports from retrieved evidence.

stories.title: IMPACTeen: Intentions, Manipulation, Persuasion, Annotations, and Consequences in Teen Communication Dataset
stories.author: Przemysław Kazienko
bullet_points: IMPACTeen is a new dataset focusing on social influence, manipulation, and persuasion in adolescent communication.
The dataset includes 1,021 texts and 5,100 annotations, labeled from five distinct expert perspectives (teenagers, parents, psychologists, etc.).
It was created using constrained LLM generation followed by rigorous human editing and validation.

stories.title: LESS Is More: Mutual-Stability Sampling for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Michalis Vazirgiannis
bullet_points: Introduces LESS, a training-free method designed to improve the efficiency of Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation of dLLMs: inefficient sampling procedures that waste computation on stable or prematurely committed tokens.
dLLMs are presented as a promising alternative to traditional autoregressive decoding due to their ability to allow parallel token updates and bidirectional conditioning.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: roman.pt via hoistbypetard
bullet_points: The story concerns a security vulnerability or 'backdoor' discovered within a LinkedIn job offer context.
This suggests a potential risk related to phishing, credential harvesting, or malicious recruitment practices on the platform.
The topic is relevant to cybersecurity and professional networking platforms, indicating a moderate but important security issue.

stories.title: How memory safety CVEs differ between Rust and C/C++
stories.author: kobzol.github.io via nrposner
bullet_points: The article compares memory safety vulnerabilities (CVEs) in Rust versus C/C++.
It addresses how the design philosophies of these two languages impact the nature of memory bugs.
This is relevant for developers and security professionals concerned with systems programming security.

stories.title: Why the Fable 5 Crisis Proves Your AI Context Layer Can't Live Inside the Model
stories.author: jon_at_backboardio
bullet_points: The article discusses the 'Fable 5 Crisis' as a case study.
It argues against embedding AI context layers directly within the core model.
The central theme is the separation of intelligence (model) from memory (context layer).

stories.title: BrowserAct vs Playwright: Where Test Automation Hits Real-World Anti-Bot Friction (Hands-On Comparison)
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: Compares BrowserAct and Playwright specifically in the context of real-world anti-bot detection.
Addresses the common development challenge of tests passing locally but failing in CI due to bot detection.
Provides a hands-on comparison relevant to QA engineers and automation practitioners.

stories.title: Your AI Provider Is a Single Point of Failure
stories.author: maishsk
bullet_points: The story discusses regulatory scrutiny of major AI providers.
The U.S. Commerce Department reportedly sent a letter to Anthropic.
The topic touches upon the risk of single points of failure within AI infrastructure.
